# Source Card: Utility of Cystatin C-based Equation for the Estimation of Glomerular Filtration Rate in a Pediatric Population

## Summary
According to PubMed, this peer-reviewed single-center retrospective study ([DOI](https://doi.org/10.1093/jalm/jfae034), PMID 38656545) validated the CKiD-under-25 (U25) eGFR equations against Tc-99m DTPA measured GFR in 57 pediatric/young-adult patients (6 months to 22 years). For the pediatric CDS module it establishes that, in this cohort, cystatin C-based equations (CKiD U25-CysC and Schwartz CysC) tracked mGFR best, while non-race-corrected creatinine equations systematically overestimated eGFR. The findings are equation-performance metrics on one small cohort, not universal clinical decision cutoffs.

## Key evidence (with exact-passage locators)

- (ev_002) NRC creatinine equations overestimated eGFR; CKiD U25-creatinine lowest bias 22.59 — locator: Abstract, Results — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:df178e664f98ea2e5b6b1cc09dee377e14a1dc0a9f4e0465ecd6954966b2b35b]"
- (ev_003) CKiD U25-CysC (0.6281, 80.7%, 3.72) and Schwartz CysC (0.6372, 77.2%, -4.68) best — locator: Abstract, Results — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:5c851fcf6b62b2ccbbe659f89b0a8be2bdc63c41b42f2900d6ef554ed92bd537]"
- (ev_004) N=57, age 6 months to 22 years, Tc-99m DTPA reference — locator: Abstract, Methods — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:67416e354066521c23457a46192a214193eb24841012412f44ef8c524d312e50]"
- (ev_005) Conclusion: CKiD U25-CysC overall best in this cohort — locator: Abstract, Conclusion — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:0d353d2ab66e8d43abe5ef7b52ad6855e5938e20711d56435bdb9ab0acd41c94]"

## Population / assay / lifecycle notes
- Population: 57 patients, 6 months to 22 years, mixed races/ethnicities, both sexes; single tertiary center (Texas Children's Hospital); mixed CKD and non-CKD referred for nuclear-medicine GFR.
- Assay/method dependence: Reference standard is Tc-99m DTPA plasma clearance (mGFR). eGFR inputs are serum cystatin C and serum creatinine; both analytes are assay/calibrator dependent, but the abstract does not specify the analyzers/methods used — a method-traceability gap for any CDS use.
- Lifecycle: Study published 2024-07 (J Appl Lab Med 9(4):803-808). The evaluated equations (CKiD U25, Schwartz cystatin C) are external published equations; this paper does not define or version them, it validates them. No effective/retire dates for a threshold are asserted here.

## Scope-exit / referral relevance (if any)
- None. The abstract reports equation-performance metrics (bias, P30, correlation) and does not define any clinical red-flag, referral GFR cutoff, or CKD-staging threshold. Any GFR staging boundary (e.g., CKD stage cutoffs) must come from a guideline source, not this study.

## Limitations & conflicts
- Full text was not available in PubMed Central; all numeric values are quoted verbatim from the abstract only, not from the Results tables — table-level per-group breakdowns and CIs are not locatable in the retrievable source.
- Small single-center retrospective cohort (N=57); performance metrics are cohort-specific and should not be treated as generalizable clinical cutoffs.
- Cystatin C and creatinine assay methods/calibrators are not stated in the abstract; because these analytes are method-dependent, the equation-performance results are not portable across labs without method-matching information (gap: analyzer/method not locatable in source).
- No numeric clinical decision threshold (referral/CKD-stage GFR cutoff) is present in this source — do NOT synthesize one from these performance metrics.
- No conflict with other sources recorded at ingestion; if a guideline source asserts a different preferred pediatric eGFR equation, preserve it as a conflict rather than collapsing.
